quote: The addon uses the Chromatic public GraphQL API. We rely on its schema to generate type definitions.
runChromaticBuild requires projectId and userToken, then calls chromatic/node run with initialize/build/upload/verify/snapshot progress handlers.
Screenshots are stored in a folder named __screenshots__ next to the story file.
The Node middleware exposes a same-origin tRPC endpoint and routes requests to server-side capture and filesystem services.
The capture service builds a story URL with args and globals, opens it in Playwright, waits for Storybook rendering, executes configured actions, and captures a page or element screenshot.
reg-suit separates working artifacts into actual, expected, and diff directories, then invokes reg-cli with thresholds, anti-alias handling, and configurable concurrency.
Storybook distinguishes UI-based addons from presets; UI addons can add panels and toolbars, while packages expose manager and preview entries.

locator: Enable visual tests, Run visual tests, Review changes sections
quote: The official workflow turns every story into a visual test, reports changed stories in the sidebar, shows pixel changes in the addon panel, and lets users accept changes as baselines locally.
Playwright page.screenshot supports disabled animations, hidden carets, full-page capture, masks, CSS-pixel scaling, injected style, and PNG output.
The addon registers a story-only toolbar and panel, but its Node path combines a sidecar HTTP server, spawned JSON-RPC CLI, Storybook channel, and SSE fallback.
Update Snapshot starts a new update-mode capture rather than promoting the exact reviewed candidate, and the result payload lacks a baseline path.
Creevey separates committed baseline images from ephemeral report artifacts and approval copies the exact selected actual image into the baseline directory.
Creevey models start, stop, approve, approveAll, status, and incremental updates through one typed protocol and supports side-by-side, swap, slide, and blend review modes.

locator: src/crawler/storybook.ts and README at SHA f08221fc2a82125df56177c2363185a886bea73a
quote: Lost Pixel provides first-class Storybook discovery from a built or running Storybook, but its OSS workflow is CLI/CI-oriented rather than an in-Storybook review surface.
Loki is purpose-built for Storybook visual regression and recommends Chrome in Docker for reproducible tests independent of the host OS.
